# Copilot Coding Agent Onboarding — android-design-system

## Overview
Infomaniak Android Design System — a Compose-based UI library providing design tokens and per-product themes. Tokens are generated from Figma via JSON and a Kotlin script. Published modules: `Foundation` (core tokens + `EsdsTheme`) and per-product `Theme*` modules (e.g. `ThemeMail`, `ThemeCalendar`).

## Build
```bash
./gradlew build          # builds all modules: Foundation, PrimitiveTokens, app (demo), catalog
./gradlew :Foundation:assembleRelease
```
No env file needed. No submodules.

## Tests & Lint
No unit tests. No standard Android CI. The only workflow is token generation:
- CI: `.github/workflows/generate-android-specific-tokens.yml` — triggers when `token-source/android_specific_tokens.json` changes; runs `scripts/generateAndroidSpecificTokens.main.kts` and auto-commits generated code.

## Token Update Workflow
1. Modify `token-source/android_specific_tokens.json`
2. CI auto-generates code and pushes to the PR branch — **do not manually edit generated token files**
3. After CI push, manually create/update the public `EsdsTheme.Values` instance in each `Theme*` module (this part is not automated)

## Project Layout
```
Foundation/             # Core theme infrastructure — EsdsTheme, token interfaces
PrimitiveTokens/        # Raw primitive token values (auto-generated)
ThemeMail/              # Mail product theme (example of Theme* module pattern)
app/                    # Demo/sample app
catalog/                # Compose component catalog
build-logic/            # Convention plugins
scripts/
└── generateAndroidSpecificTokens.main.kts   # Token generation script (Kotlin script)
token-source/
└── android_specific_tokens.json             # Source of truth for tokens — edit this, not generated files
```

## Key Rules
- **Never manually edit files in `PrimitiveTokens/`** — they are auto-generated by the CI script.
- After token generation, each `Theme*` module needs a public `EsdsTheme.Values` instance (light, dark, lightMediumContrast, lightHighContrast, darkMediumContrast, darkHighContrast).
- Use only Material3 Compose components (`androidx.compose.material3`).
- When adding/removing a runtime dependency, update `LICENSES.md` at the repo root.
